title: Sticky events delivered out of post order to late subscribers
body: When multiple sticky events of related types are posted with postSticky() before a subscriber registers, register() seems to deliver them in an order that doesn't match the original posting order, based on iteration over the stickyEvents map in EventBus.java.

title: Reflection-based subscriber lookup not cached across EventBus instances
body: SubscriberMethodFinder re-scans a subscriber class with reflection every time a new EventBus instance (not just a new subscriber) registers the same class, even though the METHOD_CACHE is presumably meant to avoid exactly this cost.

title: BackgroundPoster can reorder events under high concurrent posting
body: Under a stress test with many threads calling post() with ThreadMode.BACKGROUND handlers, some subscribers receive events in a different order than they were posted, which contradicts the sequential delivery guarantee described for the background thread mode.

title: Clarify MAIN vs MAIN_ORDERED behavior outside Android
body: ThreadMode's Javadoc explains that MAIN_ORDERED always queues for delivery while MAIN may call directly if already on the main thread, but both say 'behaves the same as POSTING' off Android — it's unclear whether that means they become fully synchronous or still go through some queue in a plain JVM app.

title: PendingPostQueue may retain references after unregister
body: After unregister() is called mid-flight, pending posts already queued for that subscriber don't appear to be purged from PendingPostQueue, which could keep the subscriber object reachable longer than expected.
